summary: Corrected the Yoshi source/object splits so the tail end of ftYs_Init.c was reassigned to ftYs_Guard.c. This moved three ftYs_Init-addressed routines from Init into Guard, adjusted ftYoshi text/sdata/sdata2 ranges, moved their declarations from ftYs_Init.h to ftYs_Guard.h, and marked ftYs_Init.c as Matching in configure.py. The PR body states that ftYs_Init became fully matching and linking after these split fixes.
what_changed: Changed ftYs_Init.c text split end from 0x8012C030 to 0x8012BDA0, shrinking Init to end at ftYs_Init_8012BDA0.
Changed ftYs_Guard.c text split start from 0x8012C030 to 0x8012BDA0, making Guard own the functions at 0x8012BDA0, 0x8012BE3C, and 0x8012BECC before ftYs_GuardOn_0_Anim at 0x8012C030.
Moved the implementations of ftYs_Init_8012BDA0, ftYs_Init_8012BE3C, ftYs_Init_8012BECC, plus helper static no-inline/sub functions, from ftYs_Init.c to ftYs_Guard.c.
Moved prototypes for ftYs_Init_8012BDA0, ftYs_Init_8012BE3C, and ftYs_Init_8012BECC from ftYs_Init.h to ftYs_Guard.h.

Adjusted ftYoshi sdata and sdata2 split ownership: ftYs_Init.sdata now ends at 0x804D3E58, ftYs_Guard.sdata owns 0x804D3E58-0x804D3E68, ftYs_SpecialS.sdata owns 0x804D3E68-0x804D3E80, ftYs_Init.sdata2 now ends at 0x804D9A28, and ftYs_Guard.sdata2 owns 0x804D9A28-0x804D9A38.
Changed the symbol metadata for ftYs_Init_804D9A30 at .sdata2:0x804D9A30 from data:double to data:8byte.
Updated configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Init.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Added an include of ft/ft_0877.h to ftYs_Guard.c, presumably required by the moved guard/init routines.
smart_moves: Fixed matching by moving code across source files rather than perturbing the function bodies, preserving the already working codegen while correcting object ownership.
Updated splits.txt, headers, source files, and configure.py together, which kept source organization, linker sections, and matching status consistent.
Used the actual address boundary 0x8012BDA0 as the split point, aligning the source split with the first moved routine instead of leaving ftYs_Init.c owning guard-related text.

Preserved matching-sensitive helper wrappers such as ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline_2 and ftYs_Init_8012B8A4_no_inline after the move, including PAD_STACK usage and the local padded Vec3 wrapper struct.
Recognized that non-text sections also had to be split, not just .text, by redistributing .sdata and .sdata2 ranges among ftYs_Init, ftYs_Guard, and ftYs_SpecialS.
Changed symbol data typing from double to 8byte for ftYs_Init_804D9A30, suggesting the matching issue was section/data-layout related rather than semantic floating-point usage.
decomp_lessons: If a file only fails matching/linking near a source-file boundary, inspect the configured split ranges before changing C code.

Function names with an old file prefix, such as ftYs_Init_8012BECC, do not guarantee the routine belongs in that file after split correction; address ownership and object layout are stronger evidence.
When moving functions between character files, move public declarations to the header that corresponds to the new object owner so consumers include the correct module boundary.
Matching status in configure.py should only be upgraded after the split configuration and source ownership make the object fully matching and linking.
Small .sdata/.sdata2 ownership mistakes can prevent an otherwise matching C file from linking as Matching.
Do not discard matching scaffolding such as no-inline wrapper functions, PAD_STACK calls, or artificial padded locals when relocating code between objects.
naming_conventions: Yoshi character files use the ftYs_ prefix, with module-style filenames such as ftYs_Init.c and ftYs_Guard.c.
Unknown or address-based functions retain address-suffixed names such as ftYs_Init_8012BDA0, ftYs_Init_8012BE3C, and ftYs_Init_8012BECC even after moving to ftYs_Guard.c.
Header prototypes are annotated with address comments, for example /* 12BDA0 */ void ftYs_Init_8012BDA0(Fighter_GObj* gobj);
Motion-state routines in Guard use descriptive state suffixes such as ftYs_GuardOn_0_Anim, ftYs_GuardOn_0_IASA, and ftYs_GuardOn_0_Phys.
Split config entries follow paths like melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Guard.c with explicit .text, .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 start/end ranges.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Corrected object split boundaries in config/GALE01/splits.txt to move a contiguous text range 0x8012BDA0-0x8012C030 from ftYs_Init.c into ftYs_Guard.c.
Retained PAD_STACK(4 * 2), PAD_STACK(4 * 4), and PAD_STACK(8) in moved functions to preserve stack layout.
Kept static no-inline trampoline helpers around ftYs_Init_8012B8A4, indicating an inlining/codegen workaround needed for matching.
Used a padded local struct containing u8 _pad[4] and Vec3 v in ftYs_Init_8012BECC_sub, a matching tactic for local stack placement/alignment.
Adjusted .sdata2 symbol metadata for ftYs_Init_804D9A30 from double to 8byte, likely to avoid incorrect typed emission while preserving binary data layout.
After the split fix, changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Init.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_Init.c").
review_feedback: The PR body says the fixes came from a group session and that ftYs_Init is now fully matching and linking.
The author noted they would wait for PR #1933 and resolve conflicts before merging.
No review comments were present in the provided slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #1933 for conflicts or related ftYoshi split changes.
Search later PRs for ftYs_Guard.c becoming Matching after this split correction.
Audit nearby ftYoshi files for remaining .sdata/.sdata2 split ownership issues.
Search for other symbols typed as double that should be data:8byte in symbols.txt.
Inspect whether address-prefixed ftYs_Init_* functions in ftYs_Guard.c were later renamed to behavior-based guard names.
